description: Compilation Step — multiple scoped prd-*.json files in tasks/ merge into a single flat prd.json before Ralph runs

userStories.title: Create tasks/ directory structure and example files
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a defined layout for tasks/ so I know exactly what files to create.
acceptanceCriteria: tasks/prd-master.json exists as a template with fields: project, branchName, description
tasks/prd-example-scope.json exists as a minimal example sub-PRD with namespaced IDs, priority, passes, and notes fields
tasks/README.md exists (stub is fine; full content covered in US-006)
Both JSON files are valid JSON
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement core merge logic in merge-prds.sh
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to run merge-prds.sh and have it produce a valid prd.json from all prd-*.json files in tasks/.
acceptanceCriteria: merge-prds.sh is executable and located in the repo root alongside ralph.sh
Script checks that jq is installed and exits 1 with a clear error if not
Reads tasks/prd-master.json for project, branchName, and description
Discovers all tasks/prd-*.json files excluding prd-master.json
Merges userStories arrays from all sub-PRDs in case-insensitive alphabetical filename order
Story IDs are namespaced using the filename stem: prd-query-optimization.json produces IDs prefixed query-optimization- (e.g. query-optimization-001)
Global priority values are reassigned 1, 2, 3... in merge order (original values ignored)
Output written to prd.json in the repo root
Script exits 0 on success, non-zero on any error
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Preserve passes status on re-merge
userStories.description: As a developer, I want re-running merge-prds.sh after Ralph has completed some stories to not reset completed work.
acceptanceCriteria: Before writing prd.json, script reads existing prd.json if it exists
For each story in the merged output, if a story with the same namespaced ID exists in the old prd.json with passes: true, the merged story inherits passes: true
New stories (not in old prd.json) default to passes: false
Stories removed from sub-PRDs are dropped from the output and not carried over
Existing prd.json with no matching stories is treated as empty with no error
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add validation with clear error messages
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the script to catch common mistakes before writing prd.json so I don't hand Ralph a broken task list.
acceptanceCriteria: Error if tasks/prd-master.json is missing or not valid JSON
Error if any tasks/prd-*.json file is not valid JSON
Error if any sub-PRD is missing the userStories array
Warning printed to stderr (not an error, exit 0) if a sub-PRD has zero user stories
Error if duplicate story IDs are detected after namespacing
All errors print the offending filename and a human-readable message to stderr
Script exits non-zero on any error, zero if only warnings
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add --dry-run flag
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to preview what the merge would produce without writing prd.json, so I can safely inspect the output before committing.
acceptanceCriteria: merge-prds.sh --dry-run runs all validation and merge logic
Merged prd.json content is printed to stdout instead of writing the file
Summary line printed to stderr: [dry-run] Would merge N stories from M files
prd.json and all other files are not modified when --dry-run is passed
Exit code follows the same rules as a normal run (non-zero on validation error)
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Write tasks/README.md workflow documentation
userStories.description: As a developer, I want clear documentation in tasks/README.md so I can onboard to the multi-PRD workflow without reading the script.
acceptanceCriteria: Explains the purpose of the compilation step in 2-3 sentences
Describes the required files: prd-master.json and prd-*.json sub-PRDs
Shows the prd-master.json schema with a concrete example
Shows the prd-*.json sub-PRD schema with a minimal example
Explains ID namespacing with a concrete example (filename to prefix to final IDs)
Documents the passes preservation behavior on re-merge
Shows standard usage: ./merge-prds.sh && ./ralph.sh
Documents the --dry-run flag with example output
Notes that prd-master.json is excluded from the merge sweep
Notes that filename prefixes (e.g. prd-01-queries.json) control merge order
Typecheck passes
